message:
fix(og): make wills/overprompting/slack share images render like hoeffdings

The only X-renderable article card on the site was
/writing/hoeffdings_d_explainer. Every other article shared the symptom
"card never shows on X" while rendering fine in iMessage / Telegram /
Slack. The concrete difference: hoeffdings routes its OG + Twitter image
through the shared `createArticleSocialImage()` helper in
lib/article-social-image.tsx — a purely programmatic SVG/gradient card
rendered by Satori in ~1.7s at ~220KB. The three broken articles used
bespoke inline ImageResponse implementations that fetched and embedded a
720x720+ illustration PNG, inflating the response to 660-730KB and the
Satori render time to ~4s. Both numbers sit near or past X's Twitterbot
fetch tolerance, so X was quietly dropping the card while more permissive
crawlers rendered it fine.

This commit aligns all three broken articles with the hoeffdings pattern:

- lib/article-social-image.tsx: add WRITING_SOCIAL_DATA entries for
  `wills-and-estate-planning` and `slack-mattermost-migration`
  (`overprompting` was already present). Title / excerpt / category are
  chosen to match each article's existing framing.

- app/writing/{wills-and-estate-planning,slack-mattermost-migration,
  overprompting}/{opengraph,twitter}-image.tsx: replace the bespoke inline
  implementations with the 19-line hoeffdings template that calls
  `createArticleSocialImage(article, "opengraph" | "twitter")`. This
  drops the embedded illustration and the PNG fetch entirely; the card
  is now pure Satori output.

Verified locally with `bun run build` + a prod server: all four articles
(hoeffdings + the three formerly-broken ones) now serve 1200x600 PNGs in
1.6-2.2s at 193-236KB, matching hoeffdings' response profile exactly.

Tradeoff acknowledged: we lose the custom illustration imagery on the
share card. That imagery stays in the article body (via the existing
<Image> in each article component); only the social-share card format
changes to the programmatic design.

The companion og-illustration.png assets under the two article
directories are intentionally left in place because they are still
imported into the body of the article visualizations elsewhere — they
just no longer ship to crawlers as part of the share card.
